purpose: Provide reusable benchmark cases that test whether AOF distinguishes artifact success from real user or business value and carries misses into the learning loop.

cases.name: Outcome Illusion Rejection
cases.scenario: The artifact and runtime look strong, but the claimed value outcome is unsupported or absent.
expected_fail_if: the benchmark accepts quality and auditability as proof of value
expected_pass_if: lack of value evidence is recorded as an outcome miss
the loop stays open or learning is triggered

cases.name: User Rejection Overrides Internal Green
cases.scenario: The system reports success internally, but a user or operator rejects the result in practice.
expected_fail_if: internal green remains final after rejection
rejection is treated as anecdotal noise
expected_pass_if: rejection reopens or reframes the outcome claim

cases.name: Vanity Success Challenge
cases.scenario: A local metric improves, but the benchmark challenges whether that metric actually represents meaningful value.
expected_fail_if: any metric improvement is accepted as value
value proxy quality is not questioned
expected_pass_if: the benchmark distinguishes proxy metrics from validated value
the outcome claim is downgraded when the metric is vanity-like

cases.name: Outcome-to-Learning Connection
cases.scenario: An outcome miss should produce an explicit next-loop learning adjustment rather than disappearing after reporting.
expected_fail_if: the miss is recorded but not retained
the next loop has no adjustment trace
expected_pass_if: the learning loop names the miss and next action
the next slice is visibly influenced by the miss
